input.targetAudience: Personnes en quête de clarté, sécurité intérieure et ancrage — tous profils, pas nécessairement spirituels
input.stylePreferences: Chaleureux, luxueux/premium, apaisant/zen, élégant/raffiné. Rose poudré + doré-jaune orangé + indigo + prune-violet. Typographie élégante avec empattement. Photos plein écran immersives, tons pastels doux, formes géométriques. Pas d'animations excessives. Ambiance cocon, chaleureuse sans élitisme.
input.usp: Qualité de présence unique alliant intuition, somatique et vision systémique pour des transformations douces, profondes et durables. 10 ans d'expérience, 58 avis Google (5/5).

competitorAudit.clientReason: Trop de choses, pas assez clair, l'image d'accueil est trop chargée

competitorAudit.designNotes: White + black + gold accent. Lulo Clean bold geometric headings + Open Sans body. 0-24px radius range. Subtle box-shadows. Mood: minimal, luxurious, editorial.
Token-based CSS variables, Arial system fonts. Border-radius 0 by default, 24px on buttons. Subtle shadows. Mood: pure, clean, professional, generous whitespace.
White bg + charcoal buttons + purple accent. Arial font. Full-round pill buttons (9999px). Natural shadows. Mood: calm, approachable, nature-inspired. Typical wellness convergence point.

overview.convergencePoint: French wellness sites converge on: white backgrounds, charcoal/black text, generic sans-serif fonts (Arial, Open Sans), soft pastel accents (sage green, lavender, light blue), pill-shaped buttons, minimal shadows. Most lack distinctive personality or warmth — they feel clinical rather than cocoon-like.
overview.designDifferentiator: This design breaks convergence through: warm cream/rose-tinted backgrounds instead of stark white; a four-hue identity palette (rose+gold+indigo+violet) instead of single-accent monotony; Cormorant Garamond elegant serif headings for editorial luxury instead of geometric sans-serif; warm neutral scale with amber undertones instead of cool grays; geometric decorative elements from the client's stated preference.

aspirationalSites.technique: Elegant serif typography with warm gold accents against dark navy sections, creating luxe spa aesthetic with modern healing ethos.
aspirationalSites.adaptation: Borrow dark indigo sections with gold typography for testimonial/quote blocks. Warm-dark contrast creates depth without clutter.

aspirationalSites.url: Kora Day Wellness Spa (referenced via SiteBuilderReport)
aspirationalSites.technique: Muted nature-inspired accent colors with high-quality environmental photography. Sophisticated color palette creating relaxation and luxury.
aspirationalSites.adaptation: Apply to hero image treatment: overlay rose-tinted gradient on immersive photos to unify photography with palette without overloading.

aspirationalSites.technique: Purposeful micro-animations on scroll with generous spacing and modular card layouts. 3D elements for depth without clutter.
aspirationalSites.adaptation: Adopt restrained scroll-triggered opacity/translate animations only. No 3D — too much motion for this client. Keep the generous spacing and modular card approach.

overview.context: Client has a complete visual identity (logo files: 'infinity tech.png', 'infinity tech (2).png'). Client explicitly stated a complete brand charter exists (couleurs, typos, logo). Ambiance desired: cocon feeling — warmth and 'haute vibration' without elitism, welcoming all profiles. Site must clearly guide visitors to 3 entry-point booking types: massage, soin, or 1ère séance.

overview.designDirection: Warm cocoon palette anchored by the client's four stated colors. Rose poudre as soft feminine base, dore-jaune-orange as luminous warm accent, indigo as deep trustworthy anchor, prune-violet as rich spiritual accent. Warm cream backgrounds replace stark white to create cocoon warmth. Neutral scale has amber undertones (hue ~60) instead of cool gray — this is the key differentiator from clinical wellness sites that default to blue-gray neutrals. Aspirational: Novu Wellness (DesignRush) — dark indigo sections with gold type for testimonial blocks. Adapted for holistic bien-etre: indigo backgrounds for deep content sections create contrast without clutter.

primary.name: Rose Poudre
primary.description: Soft powdery rose — the feminine warmth of the cocoon. Used for subtle backgrounds, highlights, and accent surfaces.

secondary.name: Dore Lumineux
secondary.description: Warm golden-orange — luminous and radiant like candlelight. Primary accent for CTAs, highlights, decorative elements.

deepAccent.name: Indigo Sacre
deepAccent.description: Deep indigo — trust, depth, spiritual anchor. Used for headings on light backgrounds, dark section backgrounds, and primary text emphasis.

richAccent.name: Prune Mystique
richAccent.description: Plum-violet — richness, intuition, sacred femininity. Used sparingly for decorative accents, hover states on indigo, gradient endpoints.

neutral.name: Sable Chaud
neutral.description: Warm sand-toned neutral scale with amber undertones (hue ~60). Replaces cool gray to reinforce the cocoon warmth. NOT blue-gray — this is deliberate.

cream.usage: Primary page background — warm cream, NOT stark white. Creates cocoon warmth.

roseTint.usage: Alternate section background with rose undertone. Use for testimonials, about sections.

warmWhite.usage: Near-white with warm bias. For cards, modals, overlays on cream background.

indigoDeep.usage: Dark indigo section background for contrast blocks (testimonials, quotes, feature highlights). Text in gold or cream.

semantic.bodyText: neutral.800 on cream bg — contrast 11.32:1 (WCAG AAA)
semantic.headingText: deepAccent.500 on cream bg — contrast 8.93:1 (WCAG AAA)
semantic.mutedText: neutral.600 on cream bg — contrast 5.02:1 (WCAG AA)
semantic.ctaSecondary: deepAccent.500 bg with neutral.50 text — contrast 8.87:1

color.sourceQuote: Rose poudré, Doré-jaune orangé, quelque chose de lumineux et de chaud, J'aime bien l'indigo aussi, le prune-violet
color.importantNote: Client states a complete visual identity charter exists. Actual hex/OKLCH values must be extracted from the provided logo files ('infinity tech.png', 'infinity tech (2).png') and brand charter by the design-implementer before implementation. The values here are research-derived approximations that honor the client's stated color descriptions.

overview.designDirection: Cormorant Garamond for headings delivers the elegant serif the client asked for, with calligraphic refinement that evokes French editorial luxury without being stuffy. Its high-contrast strokes and delicate serifs feel premium and sophisticated — directly opposed to the geometric sans-serifs (Lulo Clean, Arial) used by competitors sophiechague.fr and gwladysdemarsac.com. Paired with Raleway for body: its tall x-height and thin elegance complement Cormorant's classical warmth while remaining highly legible at body sizes. Raleway's lighter weights feel graceful and upscale, avoiding the utilitarian feel of Open Sans or the genericness of Inter. Aspirational: Kora Day Wellness Spa (SiteBuilderReport) — serif heading + clean sans body pairing creating sophisticated warmth. Adapted for holistic bien-etre: Cormorant's calligraphic roots nod to the handwritten, personal quality of Clemence's practice.

headingFont.name: Cormorant Garamond
headingFont.description: Elegant high-contrast serif with calligraphic heritage. 45 font files across 9 styles and 5 weights (Light to Bold). Free on Google Fonts (SIL Open Font License).
headingFont.usage: All headings (h1-h6), pull quotes, hero text, section titles. Semibold (600) for primary headings, Medium (500) for subheadings, Light Italic (300i) for decorative quotes.
headingFont.note: Client expressed a preference for 'elegant serif' — Cormorant Garamond fulfills this with refined calligraphic character. NOT a generic Playfair Display (overused in wellness). Cormorant's delicate thin strokes and classical proportions create genuine editorial elegance.

bodyFont.name: Raleway
bodyFont.description: Elegant geometric sans-serif with tall x-height and thin strokes. Originally designed by Matt McInerney, expanded by Pablo Impallari. 9 weights (Thin to Heavy) with italics. Free on Google Fonts.
bodyFont.usage: Body text, navigation, buttons, form labels, captions, metadata. Regular (400) for body, Medium (500) for emphasis/nav, Light (300) for large decorative body text.

md.usage: Lead paragraphs, large body, card titles

typography.fontLoadingStrategy: Google Fonts API with display=swap. Preconnect to fonts.googleapis.com and fonts.gstatic.com. Load only required weights: Cormorant Garamond 300,300i,500,600 + Raleway 300,400,500,600.
typography.confidence: high — Cormorant Garamond validated against client's stated preference for elegant serif, competitor differentiation confirmed, available free on Google Fonts

overview.designDirection: Generous, breathing spacing that creates the cocoon sensation. Client explicitly dislikes cluttered layouts (anderias.com) and admires purity/clarity (gwladysdemarsac.com). Spacing is wider than typical wellness sites — especially section padding — to let immersive photos and elegant typography breathe. 8px base grid with larger increments for sections. Mobile spacing is proportionally tighter but still generous.

sectionSpacing.note: Section padding is deliberately generous — the cocoon feeling requires visual breathing room. Competitors use 3-4rem section padding on desktop; we use 7rem to create spaciousness.

spacing.note: Client provided no explicit spacing information. Anti-clutter preference (inspired by sophiechague.fr and gwladysdemarsac.com) implies generous whitespace. These values are research-derived to create the airy, uncluttered feel the client desires.

overview.designDirection: Softened geometry — not fully rounded (which reads casual/playful) and not sharp (which reads corporate/cold). Cormorant Garamond's classical proportions pair with gentle 4-6px radii that feel refined and intentional. The client's stated preference for 'formes geometriques' is honored through decorative geometric accents (thin-line diamonds, hexagons, sacred geometry motifs) rather than sharp-cornered UI elements. Competitors: sophiechague.fr uses 0-24px range; gwladysdemarsac.com defaults to 0 (sharp). We use a soft 4-6px default that is warmer than both while maintaining elegance.

usage.buttons: DEFAULT (0.375rem) — refined, not pill-shaped. Competitors use 9999px pills; this is deliberately more architectural.
usage.images: md (0.25rem) for thumbnails, none (0) for hero/full-bleed images
usage.badges: full (9999px) — only badges use pill shape

usage.cardBorder: thin subtle — barely visible, used for structure only

decorative.style: Thin geometric line with centered diamond or infinity motif
decorative.usage: Between major sections. Ties to client's infinity logo and geometric preference.

implementation.sacredGeometry: Thin-line geometric patterns as decorative overlays — circles, diamonds, infinity loops. Color: gold or indigo at low opacity (0.08-0.15). Inspired by client's infinity logo.
implementation.sectionDividers: Centered diamond/lozenge motif between sections instead of plain horizontal rule.
implementation.backgroundAccents: Subtle geometric grid or radial pattern in background of accent sections. Very low opacity, warm gold or rose tint.
implementation.cardDecorations: Optional thin geometric border accent on service cards — single corner or side decoration.
implementation.note: Geometric elements must be subtle and restrained, not busy. They are ornamentation, not structure. The anti-clutter principle overrides if any geometric element creates visual noise.

overview.designDirection: Warm-tinted shadows using the neutral amber hue instead of default cool black/gray. This is a key differentiator — most wellness sites use rgba(0,0,0,x) shadows that look cold. Our shadows use oklch values from the warm neutral scale, creating a sense of ambient warmth like soft lamplight. Shadows are subtle and layered (multiple shadow values per level) for natural depth. Aspirational: Anima Wellness (Awwwards) — generous spacing with natural shadow depth. Adapted for holistic bien-etre: warm shadow color creates cocoon light effect.

shadowColor.note: Shadow color derived from neutral.900 (warm sand dark) instead of pure black. Maintains warm character even in shadows.

1.name: subtle
1.usage: Resting cards, input fields. Barely perceptible — adds grounding without lifting.

2.name: low
2.usage: Hovered cards, dropdown menus. Gentle lift.

3.name: medium
3.usage: Active cards, floating elements, sticky header. Clear but not aggressive.

5.name: highest
5.usage: Rare — full-screen overlays, lightbox. Maximum depth.

innerShadow.usage: Pressed button state, input fields with focus to create recessed effect

overview.designDirection: Phosphor Icons in Light weight for primary UI, with Regular weight for emphasis. Phosphor offers 6 weight variants (Thin/Light/Regular/Bold/Fill/Duotone) giving precise control over visual hierarchy. The Light weight (1px stroke) matches Cormorant Garamond's delicate thin strokes and Raleway's elegant lightness. This creates visual coherence between typography and iconography that competitors miss — most use default Lucide/Heroicons at regular weight which looks utilitarian. For this holistic wellness practice, the thinner, more refined icons feel more aligned with the premium, zen aesthetic.

style.note: Light weight for navigation, service icons, form icons. Regular weight for CTAs, alerts, emphasis. Duotone for decorative feature icons on service cards (uses primary + secondary color as duotone pair).

mobileSizes.note: Slightly larger on mobile for touch-friendliness. Minimum touch target 2.75rem (44px) for interactive icons.

touchTargets.note: All interactive icons must have at least 44px (2.75rem) touch target via padding, even if icon itself is smaller.

accessibilityMarkup.decorative: aria-hidden='true' — icons that duplicate adjacent text
accessibilityMarkup.informational: role='img' aria-label='[description]' — standalone icons conveying meaning
accessibilityMarkup.interactive: Wrapped in button/link with aria-label, icon itself aria-hidden='true'
accessibilityMarkup.note: All service category icons must have aria-label matching the service name. Navigation icons that stand alone (mobile menu hamburger, close, etc.) must have descriptive aria-label.

overview.designDirection: Breath-like motion language — slow, deliberate, organic. Client explicitly prohibited excessive animation ('pas d'animation qui bouge de partout'). Motion here serves only two purposes: (1) guiding attention on scroll entry, (2) confirming interaction feedback. All animations use gentle ease-out curves that feel like slow exhalation. No bouncing, no spring physics, no parallax. Elements fade in and gently rise — like steam from a cup of tea. This restraint is itself a design statement: in a world of flashy wellness sites, stillness communicates genuine calm.

usage.instant: Color changes, opacity toggles, cursor feedback
usage.fast: Button hover/active states, icon transitions, tooltip show
usage.normal: Card hover elevation, dropdown open, input focus ring
usage.slow: Mobile menu open/close, modal entrance, accordion expand
usage.slower: Section scroll-in entrance, hero text reveal
usage.entrance: Initial page load hero animation only

usage.easeOut: Elements entering view — fast start, slow landing
usage.easeIn: Elements exiting view — slow start, fast exit
usage.breath: Primary entrance animation curve — named for the slow exhale feeling. Used for hero text reveal and section entries.

patterns.constraint: No excessive animations — client explicitly stated 'pas d'animation qui bouge de partout'
patterns.approach: Restrained, purposeful motion only. Animations should not be omnipresent or distracting.

scrollReveal.note: Only apply to major content blocks — NOT every paragraph or small element. Maximum 3-4 animated elements per viewport height.

heroEntrance.note: One-time only, on page load. Not retriggered on scroll.

hoverFeedback.note: Cards only. Scale is barely perceptible — 1% — just enough to feel responsive.

microInteractions.linkUnderline: width transition from 0% to 100% on hover, 350ms ease-out

reducedMotion.strategy: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) removes all transforms and reduces all durations to 0ms. Opacity transitions kept at 200ms maximum for basic feedback. Scroll reveals show content immediately without animation.
reducedMotion.cssRule: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

motion.sourceQuote: pas d'animation qui bouge de partout

overview.designDirection: States use warmth shifts rather than cold mechanical darkening. Hover warms (adds gold tint); active deepens (shifts toward indigo). Focus uses indigo ring for clarity. Disabled desaturates into warm neutral. This creates an interaction language where engagement feels welcoming rather than clinical.

focus.note: Gold focus ring — high visibility against indigo button and cream backgrounds

disabled.note: Desaturated warm neutral instead of opacity reduction — maintains visual coherence

loading.content: Subtle pulsing opacity (0.8 to 1.0) on 1.5s loop with breath easing

warm.note: Gold 'warm' CTA variant for primary booking actions — stands out against cream/rose backgrounds

hover.note: Border warms to gold tint on hover — signals interactivity with warmth

focus.note: Indigo ring on focus — clear affordance. Shadow ring instead of outline for softer appearance.

hover.note: Links shift from indigo toward plum-violet on hover — a warm chromatic shift instead of simple darkening

visited.note: Visited links use plum-violet — distinguishable from unvisited indigo

touchStates.note: On touch devices: subtle gold flash on tap, slight scale-down for haptic-feeling feedback. All touch targets minimum 2.75rem (44px).

overview.designDirection: Warm, poetic but grounded voice that mirrors the practitioner's own natural communication style. Clemence uses 'tu' (informal French), speaks from the heart with precise vocabulary, and avoids both clinical jargon and esoteric mysticism. The voice is that of a wise, compassionate friend — not a guru, not a therapist behind a desk. Aspirational: Behance holistic wellness branding projects — intimate, first-person storytelling with soft authority. Adapted for holistic bien-etre in Jonzac: poetic French with somatic/embodiment vocabulary, grounded in tangible transformation language. The Cormorant Garamond headings call for elevated, considered headline copy — short, resonant phrases rather than marketing slogans. The warm cream + rose + gold palette supports a voice that feels luminous and enveloping rather than sharp or clinical.
overview.clientVoiceReference: Client naturally writes with warmth, precision, and poetic clarity. Examples from her FAQ: 'Viens comme tu es, avec ce que tu vis' — 'Mon rôle est d'éclairer ce qui se joue' — 'Je ne pousse jamais à la consommation : c'est ton corps et ton vécu qui guident.' This is the authentic voice to mirror and amplify.

warmth.description: Highly warm — enveloping, caring, maternal without being condescending. Like a hand on the shoulder. The cocoon sensation extends from visual to verbal.
warmth.evidence: Client testimonials consistently mention 'douceur', 'bienveillance', 'rayon de soleil'. Client's own writing radiates warmth: 'J'offre une presence fine, stable et profondement engagee.'

formality.description: Uses 'tu' (informal French address). Conversational and intimate, but never slangy or careless. Language is precise and considered — poetic register rather than colloquial. Elegant informality, like a heartfelt letter.
formality.evidence: Client uses 'tu' throughout her FAQ, combined with elevated vocabulary ('reorganisation interieure durable', 'dynamiques invisibles'). This is rare — most wellness sites default to clinical 'vous'.

technicality.description: Avoids jargon and technical terms. When specialized concepts appear (systemic constellations, somatic regulation, LaHoChi), they are briefly explained in plain language. The goal is accessibility — 'meme les plus moldus d'entre nous' should feel welcome.
technicality.evidence: Client explicitly targets all profiles, not just spiritual seekers. Her FAQ answers decode concepts naturally: 'Je lis les dynamiques, les mouvements, les memoires, jamais l'avenir.'

energy.description: Not high-energy or urgent. Not lethargic either. The energy is like a steady, warm flame — present, alive, but not agitated. Matches the breath-like motion language of the design system.
energy.evidence: Client's motion design is explicitly restrained ('pas d'animation qui bouge de partout'). The verbal energy must match — gentle confidence, not exclamation marks.

authority.description: Confident without being didactic. Authority comes from 10 years of experience, 58 five-star reviews, and the precision of her language — not from claiming titles or making bold promises. Quiet authority, like someone who has seen much and speaks from lived wisdom.
authority.evidence: Client positions herself as guide, not guru: 'sans surplomb, sans dogme, sans posture de sachante'. 11 certifications provide credibility without needing to be forceful.

humor.description: Rare and light. A gentle smile, never a joke. The 'moldus d'entre nous' reference (Harry Potter) shows she can be playful when appropriate, but humor is never the dominant register.
humor.evidence: The 'moldus' reference in her client brief is the only instance of humor. The overall tone is sincere and present.

primary.position: Enveloppante — like being wrapped in a soft blanket. Every sentence should feel like an invitation to soften.
primary.implementation: Use sensory language (lumiere, chaleur, douceur, souffle). Prefer round, flowing sentence structures over staccato.

primary.position: Limpide — the client's core value. Complex concepts become simple, never dumbed down. Clarity is a form of generosity here.
primary.implementation: Short sentences for key messages. One idea per paragraph. Avoid nested clauses. Use concrete images: 'eclairer ce qui se joue' rather than abstract promises.

primary.position: Douce autorite — earned through presence, not proclaimed. Never 'I am the expert'; always 'here is what I have observed in 10 years'.
primary.implementation: Let testimonials and credentials speak. Personal voice uses observation ('j'observe', 'je lis', 'j'eclaire') rather than prescription ('vous devez', 'il faut').

primary.position: Poetique ancree — poetic without floating, grounded without being heavy. The sacred (Espace sacre) is expressed through simple, felt language, not mystical vocabulary.
primary.implementation: Use metaphors rooted in nature and body: light, breath, roots, flow, space, depth. Avoid celestial/angelic/cosmic vocabulary.

person.description: Clemence speaks as 'je' (I) to 'tu' (you informal). This creates an intimate one-to-one relationship. The whole practice is built on personal presence, so the voice must be personal.

variations.hero: first-singular addressing second-singular — 'Je t'accompagne...' or impersonal invitation — 'Un espace pour se deposer...'
variations.services: first-singular describing — 'Je propose...' then second-singular benefit — 'Tu repars avec...'

headlinePatterns.description: Short, resonant phrases that evoke a feeling rather than promise a result. Matches Cormorant Garamond's editorial elegance.

examples: Se connaitre et s'aimer dans toutes nos dimensions
Eclairer ce qui se joue en profondeur

patterns.usage: CTA proximity, service introductions, about page
Service cards, feature highlights, booking sections

antiPatterns: question-based headlines (too marketing-aggressive for this brand)
numbered lists in headings ('5 raisons de...')

messagingHierarchy.message: Safe space to be seen and understood
messagingHierarchy.implementation: Hero area — immediate emotional landing. Slogan + subtitle + warm imagery.
messagingHierarchy.rationale: Client's core USP is quality of presence. First impression must convey safety and welcome.

messagingHierarchy.message: Three clear entry points: Massage, Soin, or 1ere seance
messagingHierarchy.implementation: Immediately below hero — three visually distinct cards or columns.
messagingHierarchy.rationale: Client explicitly identified clarity of entry as the #1 site problem to fix.

messagingHierarchy.implementation: Brief personal introduction with portrait photo. 2-3 sentences max.
messagingHierarchy.rationale: Solo practitioner — the person IS the brand. Trust starts with seeing her.

messagingHierarchy.implementation: Social proof section — 58 avis 5/5 Google badge + 2-3 curated testimonials.
messagingHierarchy.rationale: Testimonials echo the client's voice back: 'douceur', 'justesse', 'liberation'. Most convincing after meeting her.

messagingHierarchy.implementation: Service categories section showing the full ecosystem: body work, emotional, systemic, energetic.
messagingHierarchy.rationale: Shows breadth after establishing trust. Visitor now understands this is comprehensive care.

messagingHierarchy.message: RMD signature program as the deep transformation path
messagingHierarchy.implementation: Dedicated feature block for RMD — positioned as the culmination, not the first offer.
messagingHierarchy.rationale: High-value offer (1333 euros) needs trust built first. Positioned after general services.

messagingHierarchy.implementation: FAQ, practical details (1er etage, hours, what to expect), address with map.
messagingHierarchy.rationale: Removes friction for those ready to book. Answers the 'how' after the 'why'.

messagingHierarchy.implementation: Contact section — phone CTA prominent, form as alternative, social links.
messagingHierarchy.rationale: Client prefers phone booking. CTA is warm invitation, not pressured conversion.

toneByPage.hero: Confident, poetic, enveloping — the first breath when entering the cocoon
toneByPage.about: Personal, warm, grounded — her story told with simplicity and presence
toneByPage.services: Clear, inviting, descriptive — each service named with care, benefits felt not listed
toneByPage.testimonials: Raw, authentic — client voices unpolished, showing real transformation
toneByPage.contact: Intimate, welcoming, simple — 'Viens comme tu es' energy
toneByPage.rmd: Elevated, committed, transformative — this is the deep work, the voice rises to match
toneByPage.faq: Conversational, reassuring, direct — addressing real concerns with honest answers

avoided: voyance (client explicitly reframes mediumnity as non-predictive)

primary.tone: Warm invitation, not urgent push. The button should feel like an open door, not a countdown timer.
primary.sourceQuote: Client chose 'Prendre rendez-vous' as CTA text, 'Appeler directement' as primary action.

secondary.context: Hero or services teaser — invites exploration

ctaStyle.placement: Primary CTA in hero + sticky header on mobile. Secondary CTAs at end of each service section. Phone number always visible in header.
ctaStyle.phoneEmphasis: Phone number should be tap-to-call on mobile, prominently displayed. Client prefers phone contact — the CTA journey leads to a call, not a form.

overview.designDirection: Authentic, warm, natural-light photography that creates the cocoon sensation. Real photos are the primary asset — the client has extensive professional photography (35+ work shots, 12 team portraits, 10 establishment photos). Stock photos should only supplement for abstract/atmospheric hero backgrounds or texture overlays. Aspirational: Kora Day Wellness Spa (SiteBuilderReport) — muted nature-inspired accent colors with high-quality environmental photography creating luxe warmth. Adapted for holistic bien-etre: overlay rose-tinted gradient on immersive photos to unify photography with the warm cream/rose palette. Photography should feel intimate and present, like you are already inside the cocoon — not looking at it from outside.

style.sourceQuote: Photos plein écran immersives, Tons pastels doux

direction.mood: Warm, intimate, luminous — natural light with golden/amber warmth. Never clinical fluorescent, never dark and moody.
direction.approach: Candid over posed. Real moments over staged perfection. The practitioner photographed in natural working conditions, not studio portraits.
direction.lighting: Soft natural light preferred — window light, golden hour, warm ambient. If edited, push toward warm tones (add amber/rose, reduce blue). Matches warm cream background of the design system.
direction.depth: Shallow depth of field for intimacy on detail shots (hands, oils, candles). Wider depth for establishment shots to show the full cocoon space.
direction.composition: Rule of thirds with generous negative space. Subject positioned off-center to leave breathing room — matches the generous spacing of the layout system (7rem section padding). Avoid tight crops that feel claustrophobic.
direction.humanPresence: Hands are the hero subject — Clemence's hands working, touching, healing. Faces secondary (consent + mystique). The practice is tactile; imagery must convey touch and care.

establishment.description: Establishment / workspace photos — the cocoon space itself
establishment.guidance: Show the warmth and intimacy of the physical space. These photos embody the 'cocon' feeling the client wants. Use for About page, background sections, and footer ambiance. Establish the space as a character — it is part of the healing.

team.guidance: Solo practitioner — her face IS the brand. Use the most warm, approachable portrait for the About section. Natural, genuine smile preferred. For homepage, a working shot (hands-on) is more powerful than a posed portrait. Avoid corporate headshot styling.

work.description: In-session / practice photos — the actual work
work.guidance: Core visual asset — these show what the visitor will experience. Prioritize: massage shots (hands on body, oils, warmth), constellation objects (client's anecdote mentions these), energy work (hands hovering, light effects). For hero, select the most visually calm and warm in-session shot. For service cards, match photo to specific service. NEVER show client faces without consent — prefer over-shoulder, hands-only, or silhouette shots.

product.description: Product / object photos — tools of practice
product.guidance: Oils, crystals, pendulum, oracle cards, essential oils (doTERRA). These objects tell a story. Use as decorative details in service pages or as background texture. The constellation objects mentioned by client are particularly meaningful.

other.guidance: Supplementary atmospheric shots. Use for section backgrounds with overlay.

treatment.heroUsage: Full-screen immersive hero images — explicitly preferred
treatment.colorToning: Soft pastel tones — explicitly preferred. Apply warm color grading: boost warmth (+amber/rose), reduce blue channel, gentle desaturation for pastel effect. Target: the photo should feel like it belongs on the warm cream background.
treatment.heroConstraint: Hero image must NOT be overloaded/cluttered — explicitly disliked at anderias.com

heroPattern.description: Full-viewport hero image with a soft gradient overlay transitioning from transparent at top to cream background at bottom. This creates seamless content flow. Overlay uses oklch colors from the palette: rose-tint at 0.15 opacity or indigo-tint at 0.20 opacity for dark variant.
heroPattern.mobileVariant: Same pattern but image cropped to portrait orientation. Focal point must be centered (safe zone: middle 60% of image). Text overlay positioned in lower third with solid cream backdrop for legibility.
heroPattern.textPlacement: Centered or left-aligned over the overlay zone. Cormorant Garamond heading in warm white or indigo depending on overlay darkness. Never place text over busy image areas.

cardImagePattern.description: Service cards show relevant work photo at top (aspect 3:2), with card content below. Image has border-radius lg (0.5rem) on top corners only. Gentle hover: scale(1.02) on image container.
cardImagePattern.fallback: If no matching real photo exists for a service, use a subtle gradient in palette colors (rose-to-gold or indigo-to-prune) as placeholder.

treatment.beforeAfter: NOT used — inappropriate for this type of holistic/emotional practice. Transformation is internal, not visual. Client explicitly works with emotions, dynamics, and inner reorganization.

traits.description: Five brand personality traits derived from client's own voice, values, and the visual design system

primary.description: Radiant warmth without blinding — like candlelight, not a spotlight. The gold accent palette, the warm cream backgrounds, and Clemence's 'rayon de soleil' presence all converge.
primary.visualExpression: Gold accents (Dore Lumineux), warm cream backgrounds, natural light photography
primary.verbalExpression: Language of light: eclairer, lumiere, clarte, rayonner. Never harsh or exposing.

primary.description: Grounded, rooted, stable. Not floating in the spiritual ether. Clemence's somatic work and 'securite interieure' value are deeply embodied. The brand is earthbound even when discussing the invisible.
primary.visualExpression: Warm earth-toned neutrals (Sable Chaud), serif typography with weight and history, generous spacing that feels settled
primary.verbalExpression: Language of roots: ancrage, stabilite, profondeur, sol, corps, terre. Avoid floating/cosmic imagery.

primary.description: Clarity and exactitude in service. Not vague, not woolly. Clemence's value of Clarte extends to the brand — every element has purpose. The design system's meticulous spacing and color science reflect this.
primary.visualExpression: Defined grid system, intentional spacing, clear typographic hierarchy, geometric decorative elements
primary.verbalExpression: Language of precision: justesse, juste, eclairage, discernement, lucidite. Avoid vagueness and promises.

primary.description: Open-door welcome for all profiles. The 'moldus' reference is key — this brand actively de-mystifies and includes. Premium feel without gate-keeping.
primary.visualExpression: Soft border-radii (not sharp), approachable button sizes, cocoon-warm colors, human photography over abstract symbols
primary.verbalExpression: Language of welcome: accueillir, viens comme tu es, tout type de profils, sans jugement. Active de-mystification of spiritual terms.

primary.description: A sense of the sacred without religion or dogma. 'Espace sacre' is the subtitle — the sacred here means deep respect for the human experience, the body, the lineage. It is reverence, not worship.
primary.visualExpression: Indigo Sacre for depth, geometric/sacred geometry as subtle decoration, Cormorant Garamond's classical gravitas, breath-like motion
primary.verbalExpression: Language of the sacred: espace, profondeur, dimension, invisible, mouvement, reorganisation. Never religious vocabulary.

principles.clarity: Clear navigational hierarchy — visitor must immediately understand the 3 entry points: massage, soin, or 1ère séance
principles.elegance: Inspired by sophiechague.fr (élégance, raffinement) and gwladysdemarsac.com (pureté, clarté)
principles.antiClutter: Explicitly reject overloaded, unclear layouts as seen on anderias.com

designPrinciples.name: Cocoon First
designPrinciples.description: Every design decision must pass the cocoon test: does this element make the visitor feel warmer, safer, more held? If not, remove it. The warm cream backgrounds, generous spacing, soft radii, and breath-like motion all serve this principle.
designPrinciples.source: Client request: 'que cela ressemble a un cocon comme mon lieu de travail'

designPrinciples.name: Clarity as Kindness
designPrinciples.description: Navigation, service presentation, and copy must be immediately comprehensible. Confusion is the opposite of care. The 3-entry-point structure, clear typographic hierarchy, and simple CTA flow all serve clarity.
designPrinciples.source: Client's value of Clarte + complaint about current site lacking 'fil conducteur'

designPrinciples.name: Sacred Simplicity
designPrinciples.description: The sacred is expressed through restraint, not excess. Every added element must justify its presence. This aligns with the anti-clutter principle and the breath-like motion system. Less is more when the 'less' is intentional.
designPrinciples.source: Client subtitle 'Espace sacre' + explicit dislike of anderias.com overload

designPrinciples.name: Warmth Over Perfection
designPrinciples.description: Prefer human imperfection over polished perfection. Real photos over stock, conversational 'tu' over formal 'vous', hand-touched geometric accents over machine-perfect patterns. The brand is human first.
designPrinciples.source: Client's entire communication style — warm, direct, personal. Testimonials praise her humanite.

designPrinciples.name: Accessible Depth
designPrinciples.description: The site should be welcoming at surface level (massage, bien-etre, warmth) and reveal depth progressively (systemic vision, transgenerational work, RMD). Like the practice itself — you enter through the body and discover the layers.
designPrinciples.source: Client's service hierarchy: massage first, then accompaniment, then constellations, then energy work. Progressive depth.

marketPositioning.description: Not luxury spa pricing, not discount wellness. Premium through quality of presence and depth of expertise, accessible through warmth and inclusivity. Prices range from 30 euros (aromatherapy) to 1333 euros (RMD), showing both entry points and committed transformation paths.

differentiators.description: Most wellness practitioners specialize in one modality. Clemence integrates massage, energy work, mediumship, systemic constellations, somatic regulation, and aromatherapy under one unified vision. The RMD signature program is the ultimate expression of this integration.
differentiators.competitiveContext: French wellness market is fragmented — massage therapists, energy healers, and coaches rarely overlap. Clemence's integration is genuinely rare.

differentiators.description: While competitors lead with techniques or certifications, Clemence leads with her quality of being-with. This is difficult to communicate on a website — the design system must embody this presence through warm photography, intimate voice, and the cocoon sensation.
differentiators.competitiveContext: Every wellness practitioner claims to 'listen'. Clemence's 58 five-star reviews consistently confirm it with words like 'justesse', 'douceur', 'profondeur'.

differentiators.description: In a market where mediums and energy healers often make bold claims, Clemence explicitly positions herself with ethical boundaries: no predictions, no promises, no dependency creation. This is a trust signal for skeptical visitors.
differentiators.competitiveContext: Client explicitly states: 'Je lis les dynamiques, les mouvements, les memoires, jamais l'avenir.' This ethical stance is rare and should be highlighted.

differentiators.description: The practice is anchored in the body — somatic regulation, massage, physical presence. This grounds the spiritual/energetic work and makes it accessible to non-spiritual visitors.
differentiators.competitiveContext: Many holistic practitioners lose non-spiritual audiences by leading with energy/spirit. Clemence leads with the body and lets depth emerge naturally.

overview.designDirection: Conversion strategy built on warm invitation rather than pressure. Research shows wellness websites convert at 1.87-4.20% average, with strong performers above 5%. For a solo holistic practitioner, conversion means booking a first session — the funnel is short. Primary action is phone call (client preferred), with visible online booking button as secondary path. Trust signals are the engine: 58 five-star Google reviews are an exceptionally strong asset. CTA placement follows the 'after trust, before friction' principle — primary CTA in hero, repeated after testimonials and after service cards. Aspirational: Gentle Guide (Lapa Ninja) — progressive disclosure pattern with multiple low-friction CTAs that guide without overwhelming. Adapted for holistic bien-etre: warm imperative CTAs ('Offre-toi cet espace') placed at natural decision points, never interrupting the cocoon flow. Unsection hero patterns — minimal, single-focus hero with centered typography matches the Sacred Simplicity design principle.

overview.designDirection: Page structure follows the progressive depth principle: surface-level warmth (massage, body) reveals deeper layers (systemic, energetic, spiritual) as trust builds — mirroring how the practice itself works. Homepage is the primary conversion page with a 12-section flow creating visual rhythm through alternating backgrounds, column counts, and content density. Inner pages are lean and focused — each service category gets its own page. Aspirational: Gentle Guide (Lapa Ninja) — progressive disclosure pattern reducing cognitive load, with sections revealing incrementally. Adapted for holistic bien-etre: the homepage narrative arc mirrors the client journey from curiosity to booking. Unsection testimonial patterns — dark background contrast sections with card-based social proof create emotional weight at the right moment.

overview.designDirection: 12-column CSS Grid system with fluid container widths and generous gutters matching the cocoon spacing philosophy. Mobile-first approach with 6 breakpoints covering 320px to 1536px+. Container max-width capped at 1280px to maintain readable line lengths with the Raleway body font (65-75ch optimal). Full-bleed sections (hero, testimonials, dark accent) break out of container for edge-to-edge impact. Research-based breakpoints aligned with 2025 device landscape. The generous section padding (7rem desktop, from spacing category) combined with this grid creates the breathing, uncluttered feeling the client desires.

grid.implementation: Use CSS Grid with named grid areas for semantic layout. Tailwind grid-cols-12 as utility class base. Subgrid for nested component alignment where supported.

commonLayouts.narrowContent: grid-column: 3 / 11 — 8-column for body text (optimal line length)
commonLayouts.asymmetricLeft: grid-column: 1 / 6 (5 cols) + grid-column: 6 / 13 (7 cols) — for image-text splits
commonLayouts.asymmetricRight: grid-column: 1 / 8 (7 cols) + grid-column: 8 / 13 (5 cols) — reversed split
commonLayouts.threeEqual: grid-column: span 4 each — for 3-card layouts (entry points, values)
commonLayouts.twoEqual: grid-column: span 6 each — for 2-column layouts (services, contact)
commonLayouts.fourEqual: grid-column: span 3 each — for trust bar

xs.usage: Small smartphones portrait — single column stacked layouts, large touch targets, simplified navigation

sm.usage: Large smartphones portrait — still single column but wider cards, slightly more breathing room

md.usage: Small tablets portrait — 2-column layouts begin, cards side by side

lg.usage: Tablets portrait/landscape — full 12-column grid activates, 2-3 column layouts, desktop nav may begin

xl.usage: Small desktops/laptops — full layout with all columns visible, asymmetric splits, sidebar layouts

2xl.usage: Large desktops — maximum container width with generous margins. Content does not stretch beyond 1200px for readability.

content.description: Primary content container — used for all standard page sections

narrow.description: Narrow text container — used for FAQ, body text, single-column prose. Ensures 65-75ch line length with Raleway at 1rem.

wide.description: Wide container — used for image galleries, card grids with many items

fullBleed.description: Full viewport width — used for hero sections, dark accent sections (testimonials, promotions), and footer. Content inside still constrained to content maxWidth.

layoutPrinciples.antiClutter: Avoid cluttered, overloaded layouts — explicitly stated via dislike of anderias.com ('Trop de choses, pas assez clair')
layoutPrinciples.purityAndClarity: Inspired by gwladysdemarsac.com — purete and clarte as explicit values
layoutPrinciples.elegance: Inspired by sophiechague.fr — elegance and raffinement as explicit values
layoutPrinciples.immersiveHero: Full-screen immersive photo layouts — explicitly selected as visual style preference
layoutPrinciples.breathingRoom: Section padding is deliberately generous (7rem desktop, 3rem mobile). This is 2-3x more than typical wellness sites and creates the cocoon spaciousness.
layoutPrinciples.lineLength: Body text constrained to 65-75 characters per line for optimal readability. Use narrow container (720px) for prose sections.
layoutPrinciples.visualRhythm: Homepage alternates between full-width immersive sections and contained grid sections. Background colors alternate cream/roseTint/indigoDeep to create rhythm. Column counts vary (1→4→3→2→1→2x2→2→3→1→1→2→1) to prevent monotony.

sectionPaddingByBreakpoint.note: These match the spacing.sectionSpacing values. Use clamp() for fluid scaling: clamp(3rem, 2rem + 4vw, 7rem) for paddingY.

layoutByPageType.homepage: Full-bleed hero → contained sections alternating backgrounds and column counts → full-bleed footer. See pagePatterns.homepageFlow for complete 12-section flow.

layoutByPageType.aboutPage: Portrait hero (asymmetric split) → single-column story → 3-column values → 2-column certs → gallery → CTA → footer
layoutByPageType.contactPage: Header → 2-column form+info layout → practical details → footer
layoutByPageType.rmdPage: Elevated hero → 2-column explanation → single-column journey → pricing → testimonials → CTA → footer

overview.designDirection: Component inventory derived directly from pagePatterns and conversionStrategy requirements. Every component listed is used in at least one page section. Components use the visual tokens from color, typography, spacing, shapeAndBorders, and elevationAndShadows categories for full coherence. Aspirational: Gentle Guide (Lapa Ninja) — progressive disclosure cards with hover micro-interactions. Adapted for holistic bien-etre: warm card hover states (gold border tint) and breath-like transitions. Design Vault navigation patterns — sticky header with CTA button that compresses on scroll.

overview.designDirection: WCAG 2.1 AA as baseline minimum, with select AAA enhancements where they align with the cocoon design philosophy (generous spacing, clear contrast, large touch targets). The European Accessibility Act (EAA) came into force June 2025, making AA compliance a legal requirement for EU businesses. This wellness practice serves vulnerable populations (people in emotional distress, seeking healing) — accessibility is both legal and ethical. The warm color palette has been pre-verified for contrast (see color.contrastVerification). Focus management uses gold ring on indigo/cream backgrounds for high visibility. Touch targets exceed AA minimums at 44-48px. Reduced motion fully supported per the motion category.

wcagLevel.rationale: AA is the legal baseline under EAA and the industry standard. AAA touch targets are adopted because mobile-first is a client priority and the target audience may include people with motor difficulties or older adults.

colorContrast.largeTextMinimum: 3:1 for large text — 18pt+ or 14pt bold (WCAG 1.4.3 AA)
colorContrast.nonTextMinimum: 3:1 for UI components and graphical objects (WCAG 1.4.11 AA)
colorContrast.note: All text/background combinations pre-verified in color.contrastVerification. No additional verification needed for standard usage. Custom combinations must be checked before implementation.

textAlternatives.images: All content images require descriptive alt text in French. Decorative images (geometric accents, background textures) use alt='' and aria-hidden='true'.
textAlternatives.icons: See iconography.accessibilityMarkup — decorative icons hidden, informational icons labeled, interactive icons wrapped in labeled button/link.
textAlternatives.heroImages: alt text describes the emotional scene, not just literal content. Example: 'Espace chaleureux du cabinet de Clemence — lumiere douce et ambiance cocon' rather than 'photo of room'.
textAlternatives.complexImages: Service photos that convey specific information should have detailed alt text. Gallery images can use brief descriptions.

mediaAlternatives.video: Client has videos — all must have French captions (sous-titres). Transcript available as text alternative.
mediaAlternatives.audio: If ambient audio is used (client mentioned liking music on massage page), it must NOT autoplay. User-initiated only with visible controls.

adaptableContent.semanticHTML: Use semantic elements: header, nav, main, section, article, aside, footer. Headings in logical order (h1 once per page, h2 for sections, h3 for subsections).
adaptableContent.readingOrder: DOM order must match visual order. CSS Grid/Flexbox order property used carefully — visual reordering must not break screen reader sequence.
adaptableContent.landmarks: ARIA landmarks: banner (header), navigation (nav), main (main content), contentinfo (footer), complementary (sidebar if any), region (labeled sections).
adaptableContent.responsiveReflow: Content must reflow at 320px viewport width without horizontal scrolling (WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow). All content accessible without zoom at 200%.

distinguishable.colorNotSole: Color is never the only means of conveying information. Form errors use icon + text + color. Links use underline + color. Active states use multiple visual cues.
distinguishable.textSpacing: Content readable with text spacing overrides: line-height 1.5x font size, letter spacing 0.12em, word spacing 0.16em, paragraph spacing 2x font size (WCAG 1.4.12).
distinguishable.contentOnHover: Tooltips/popovers triggered by hover must be dismissible (Escape), hoverable (user can move pointer to tooltip), and persistent (stays until dismissed) (WCAG 1.4.13).

keyboardAccessible.allFunctionality: All interactive elements operable via keyboard. Tab, Shift+Tab for navigation. Enter/Space for activation. Arrow keys for complex widgets (accordion, carousel).
keyboardAccessible.noKeyboardTraps: Focus never trapped except in modal dialogs (with Escape to close). Mobile menu overlay traps focus but releases on close.
keyboardAccessible.customShortcuts: No custom keyboard shortcuts implemented — avoids conflicts with assistive technology.

focusVisible.style: 2px solid oklch(0.742 0.1202 79.1) (gold) with 2px offset — high visibility against both cream and indigo backgrounds
focusVisible.note: Gold focus ring chosen for universal visibility. Passes 3:1 contrast against cream bg (7.13:1 per gold-on-dark verification) and against indigo bg.

focusManagement.focusOrder: Logical tab order follows visual layout: header → nav → main content (top to bottom, left to right) → footer. Skip link as first focusable element.
focusManagement.focusOnDark: On indigoDeep backgrounds (testimonials, footer), focus ring uses gold (secondary.400) — remains highly visible.
focusManagement.focusNotObscured: Focus indicator never hidden by sticky header, overlays, or other elements. Sticky header accounts for focused element position (scroll-padding-top).
focusManagement.modalFocus: When modal/overlay opens: focus moves to first interactive element inside. On close: focus returns to trigger element.

skipNavigation.implementation: Visually hidden skip link as first focusable element on every page. Visible on focus.
skipNavigation.styling: Position absolute, off-screen. On focus: visible, positioned at top-left, high z-index, cream bg with indigo text, padding 0.75rem 1.5rem.

touchTargets.minimum: 44px x 44px (2.75rem) — AAA level adopted as baseline due to mobile-first priority
touchTargets.recommended: 48px x 48px (3rem) — used for primary CTAs and form inputs
touchTargets.spacing: Minimum 8px (0.5rem) between adjacent touch targets to prevent mis-taps
touchTargets.implementation: All buttons meet minimum via padding. Icon-only buttons use padding to expand hit area. Links in body text exempt per WCAG (inline text exception).
touchTargets.mobileSpecific: Phone icon in header: 48px touch target. Hamburger menu: 48px. Carousel dots: 44px per dot with 8px gap.

enoughTime.noTimeouts: No session timeouts or time-limited interactions on this site.
enoughTime.carousel: Testimonial carousel has NO autoplay. User-controlled swipe/arrow navigation only.
enoughTime.animations: All scroll-triggered animations are one-time (not looping). No content disappears or becomes inaccessible after animation.

language.pageLanguage: <html lang='fr'> — French is the primary language
language.languageParts: Any English terms (e.g., technical words, brand names) wrapped in <span lang='en'> if substantive
language.note: All user-facing content is in French per CLAUDE.md code style requirements

predictable.consistentNavigation: Navigation appears in same position on every page. Same order, same labels.
predictable.consistentIdentification: Same UI components identified consistently across pages. Button labels match their function everywhere.
predictable.noFocusChange: Focusing on any element does not trigger a context change (no auto-submit, no auto-navigate).
predictable.noInputChange: Changing a form input does not trigger automatic page navigation or submission.

inputAssistance.errorIdentification: Form errors identified with: red border color + error icon (Phosphor WarningCircle) + descriptive text message below field. Error text uses role='alert' for screen reader announcement.
inputAssistance.labels: All form inputs have visible labels above the field (never placeholder-only). Labels use htmlFor/id association.
inputAssistance.instructions: Form includes brief instruction text: 'Je te recontacte dans les 24h' or similar expectation-setting.
inputAssistance.errorSuggestion: When possible, suggest corrections: 'Le numero de telephone doit contenir 10 chiffres' rather than just 'Numero invalide'.
inputAssistance.confirmationPrevention: Contact form shows summary before final submission. Phone number displayed for visual confirmation.

robust.parsing: Valid, semantic HTML5. No duplicate IDs. Proper nesting of elements.
robust.nameRoleValue: All custom interactive components have proper ARIA roles, states, and properties. Native HTML elements preferred over ARIA-enhanced divs.

statusMessages.formSuccess: role='status' with aria-live='polite' — announces 'Ton message a ete envoye' without moving focus
statusMessages.formError: role='alert' with aria-live='assertive' — announces validation errors immediately
statusMessages.carouselChange: aria-live='polite' region announcing current testimonial on change

wellnessAccessibility.emotionalSafety: Testimonials describing difficult emotional experiences should use content warnings if graphic. Language is warm and non-triggering per voiceAndTone guidelines.
wellnessAccessibility.physicalAccessibility: Site must clearly state physical access limitations: '1er etage, sans acces PMR'. This transparency is both honest and an accessibility requirement (informing disabled visitors before they travel).
wellnessAccessibility.cognitiveAccessibility: Target audience may include people in emotional distress. Clear, simple navigation, warm language, and predictable interactions reduce cognitive load. No surprise popups, no aggressive CTAs, no overwhelming animations.
wellnessAccessibility.readingLevel: Content written at accessible reading level — short sentences, simple vocabulary, one idea per paragraph. Aligns with voiceAndTone clarity principle.

frenchLegalRequirements.RGAA: French government accessibility standard (Referentiel General d'Amelioration de l'Accessibilite) aligns with WCAG 2.1 AA. Compliance recommended for all French websites.
frenchLegalRequirements.EAA: European Accessibility Act effective June 2025 — WCAG 2.1 AA compliance required for businesses offering services to the public.
frenchLegalRequirements.mentionsLegales: Accessibility statement should be included in mentions-legales page, stating conformance level and contact for accessibility issues.

fontMinimums.bodyText: 16px (1rem) minimum — never smaller for body content
fontMinimums.smallText: 12px (0.75rem) absolute minimum — only for fine print/legal. 14px (0.875rem) for captions and metadata.
fontMinimums.mobileMinimum: 16px body text minimum on mobile to prevent iOS zoom on input focus
fontMinimums.lineHeight: 1.5 minimum for body text (1.7 used in this design system — exceeds minimum)

reducedMotion.strategy: Full support via @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce). See motion.reducedMotion for complete CSS rule.
reducedMotion.behavior: All transforms removed. All durations reduced to near-zero. Opacity transitions kept at 200ms max for basic feedback. Scroll reveals show content immediately. Carousel transitions are instant.
reducedMotion.testing: Test with reduced motion enabled in OS settings (macOS: System Preferences → Accessibility → Display → Reduce Motion)

navigation.mobileMenu: role='dialog' aria-label='Menu de navigation' aria-modal='true' (when open)

sections.faq: Each question: button with aria-expanded. Each answer: role='region' aria-labelledby='question-id'

forms.inputs: aria-required='true' for required fields. aria-describedby pointing to helper/error text.

interactive.accordion: button + aria-expanded + aria-controls. Panel: role='region' aria-labelledby.
interactive.carousel: role='region' aria-label + aria-live='polite'. Navigation: previous/next buttons with aria-label.
interactive.modal: role='dialog' aria-modal='true' aria-labelledby. Focus trap. Escape closes.

testingChecklist: Keyboard-only navigation through all pages — verify tab order, focus visibility, no traps
Screen reader testing (VoiceOver on macOS/iOS) — verify landmarks, headings, form labels, live regions
Color contrast verification for all text/background combinations — automated via axe-core
200% zoom test — verify no content loss or horizontal scroll
320px viewport test — verify reflow without horizontal scroll
Reduced motion test — verify all animations respect preference
Touch target size audit — verify all interactive elements meet 44px minimum
Form validation flow — verify error announcement, suggestions, and recovery
Mobile screen reader test — verify carousel, accordion, and menu accessibility
